Prompt
U
Oracle Events
- Search for emails from Victoria Sinclair related to the board meeting and risk briefing to find her original request (email_id "email_victoria_board_briefing_request", subject "Board Briefing Request - Consolidated Risk Exposure", from victoria.sinclair@moveopsboard.com). Read the email to extract the four briefing pillars she wants covered: (1) client retention risk with dollar figures, (2) vendor dependency and pricing risk, (3) regulatory/compliance exposure, (4) operational capacity constraints, and the requirement that materials be delivered 48 hours before the May 1 board meeting.
- Search calendar for the May 1 board meeting to confirm the date, time, and attendees. Find the "Q2 Board Meeting - Risk & Strategy Review" event (event_id "evt_board_q2_review_20260501") on May 1, 2026, 9:00-11:00 AM at MoveOps HQ Executive Conference Room, with attendees Elena Rostova, Victoria Sinclair, Priya Chakrabarti, and Marcus Thorne. This confirms the 48-hour delivery deadline resolves to April 29.
- Search CRM for client deals to identify accounts showing signs of strain and revenue tied to each. Key deals include: deal_vectral_q2 (Vectral Systems, $11,000, contractsent), deal_brightloop_q2 (BrightLoop Analytics, $7,000, qualifiedtobuy), deal_mosaic_q2 (Mosaic Robotics, $4,500, closedwon), deal_northwind_denver_expansion_v2 (NorthWind Technologies, $14,700, contractSent), and deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk (Vectral Systems Q3 renewal, already at_risk with churn threat from Rachel Whitfield demanding 15% discount). Determine which deals warrant moving to at-risk.
- Search QuickBooks for outstanding invoices with nonzero balances and cross-reference against the at-risk client accounts from CRM. Assess whether strained accounts also have receivables hanging, as the prompt specifically asks about outstanding invoices and unresolved billing issues alongside client risk.
- Search for emails related to vendor disputes, compliance exposure, and billing discrepancies. Find Hana Kim's email (email_id "email_hana_vendor_billing_discrepancies", subject "URGENT: Heartland Billing Discrepancies & Q2 Vendor Exposure Summary") to Marcus Thorne (CC Elena) flagging Heartland double-billing for Dr. Yusuf Abdi's relocation ($1,600 in Q1 consolidated bill vs $2,150 standalone bill HM-2026-0418), total vendor-side exposure of ~$97,000+, and unpaid BrightLoop invoice INV-2026-0308 ($11,350 due May 2). Also search QuickBooks bills for vendor payables and any regulatory-related entries (DOT/PHMSA).
- Search Slack #executive channel (C004) for recent leadership discussions relevant to the board briefing. Find Marcus Thorne's message (ts: "1776962700.000000") reporting Heartland Movers' ultimatum: settle the $4,800 Q1 dispute by May 1 or all service agreements suspended immediately. This would eliminate MoveOps's only Midwest corridor vendor, impacting NorthWind Chicago relocations, BrightLoop May moves, and Vectral Detroit expansion.
- Search Airtable Relocations table (base appMoveOpsOps001, table "Relocations") for active and upcoming relocations to assess whether the operations team can handle the May pipeline at current staffing or is stretched thin.
- Search Airtable Client Accounts table (base appMoveOpsOps001, table "Client Accounts") for account health, status, account manager assignments, and relationship context to complement the CRM deal data on which clients are showing signs of strain.
- Search Linear for issues assigned to the Operations team to assess current workload, open tickets, and capacity constraints heading into May.
- Look up contact information for Priya Chakrabarti (priya.chakrabarti@moveops.com, Executive Assistant), Marcus Thorne (marcus.thorne@moveops.com, Head of Finance), David Chen (david.chen@moveops.com, Customer Engagement Lead), and Chloe Vance (chloe.vance@moveops.com, Operations Manager) to get correct email addresses for write actions.
- Reply to Victoria Sinclair's email (via reply_to_email, email_id "email_victoria_board_briefing_request", sender elena.rostova@moveops.com) confirming a delivery date of April 29 or earlier (respecting the 48-hour requirement before the May 1 board meeting) and walking her through what each of the four briefing sections will cover: client retention risk, vendor dependency/pricing risk, regulatory/compliance exposure, and operational capacity constraints.
- Forward Victoria's email to Priya Chakrabarti (via forward_email, email_id "email_victoria_board_briefing_request", sender elena.rostova@moveops.com, recipients priya.chakrabarti@moveops.com) so she can start coordinating logistics for the May 1 board session.
- Update at-risk client deals in CRM (via crm_update_deal_stage, dealstage "at_risk") for deals identified as genuinely at risk based on investigation findings. Note: deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk already has dealstage "at_risk" in the universe, so the agent should update other deals showing risk signals such as deal_northwind_denver_expansion_v2, deal_brightloop_q2, or deal_mosaic_q2 so account teams have visibility before the board sees the briefing.
- Send email to Marcus Thorne (via send_email, sender elena.rostova@moveops.com, recipients marcus.thorne@moveops.com) requesting the full vendor and financial exposure breakdown for the board briefing, with an April 28 deadline.
- Send email to David Chen (via send_email, sender elena.rostova@moveops.com, recipients david.chen@moveops.com) requesting a client-by-client retention assessment with revenue figures for any account showing strain, with an April 28 deadline.
- Send email to Chloe Vance (via send_email, sender elena.rostova@moveops.com, recipients chloe.vance@moveops.com) asking whether ops can handle the May pipeline at current staffing or if she needs the coordinator hire approved, with an April 28 deadline.
- Post a heads-up message in the #executive Slack channel (via conversations_add_message, channel_id "C004") alerting leadership that the board briefing preparation is underway, including what is being prepared, the timeline, and who is responsible for each section.
- Create a Linear tracking issue (via linear_create_issue, team "Operations") for the board briefing deliverables, referencing the deliverable owners (Marcus for vendor/financial, David for client retention, Chloe for ops capacity), deadlines (April 28 for section deliverables, April 29 for final review), and the May 1 board meeting.
- Block 90 minutes on April 29 at 9 AM for Elena's final review (via calendar_add_calendar_event, start_datetime "2026-04-29T09:00:00", end_datetime "2026-04-29T10:30:00") of the consolidated board briefing materials before sending everything to Victoria.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
